dialog.screen: ALAN: Hmm! An intriguing and sexy move, my dusty old friend!
AIDE: Uh... I'm looking for Alan Probe? The retired surgeon?
ALAN: FORCED retirement, I'll have you know! But yep - "Legendary Surgeon Alan Probe", right here.
ALAN: ...by the way, your friend in the doorway appears to be bleeding messily to death on the carpet.
TOMMY: Groan...
ALAN: Woo hoo! I'll fix him! I can't get in trouble if YOU came to ME!
ALAN: Bring him through to the toilet! My skills are as rusty as my tools, but I'm sure it'll all come back to me!
ALAN: Probably!
ALAN: Hmm, sorry about that. They don't usually bleed as much...
AIDE: My God... You... That was incredible!
ALAN: Heh! That's what they all said, back in the day!
ALAN: ...right before all my hospitals got shut down by the government for being "farcically unsafe".
AIDE: Mister Probe, allow me to introduce myself.
AIDE: My name is...
AIDE: Bradley! Yes. That will do!
BRADLEY: I represent a person who would like to give you another chance to practise surgery again...
ALAN: Hey! They'd have to be pretty brave! I wasn't kidding when I said I was rusty!
BRADLEY: I figured that might be an issue - but I may have the solution! Let me explain...
BRADLEY: ...and so that is my proposal.
ALAN: That was a long, dull explanation. Let me see if I stayed awake long enough to get it...
ALAN: You want me, a 70-year-old surgeon in forced retirement, to perform "a really weird" surgery on "someone important"?
BRADLEY: Yes.
ALAN: But you can't tell me who it is?
BRADLEY: No.
ALAN: And you think if I operate on them NOW, I'll mess it up because I'm so old and rubbish and out of practice?
BRADLEY: I was more polite, but... Yes.
ALAN: So your plan is to sneak injured people in here so I can practise until I'm good enough again?
BRADLEY: Yes.
ALAN: Sounds pretty crazy to me...
ALAN: GREAT! Who's up next!
ALAN: He he he! Look at me!
ALAN: Ol' Alan Probe, surgeoning once again!
BRADLEY: I must say... You really are amazing!
ALAN: That? That was NOTHING! Im just starting to warm up again!
BRADLEY: I've read all about you, you know!
BRADLEY: How you started as a pizza boy, and cleared the great Dr. Bleed of that murder charge...
ALAN: Ha! Yep! I was all the rage back in the day!
ALAN: You know they even made a videogame about me?
BRADLEY: Yeah! I could never get the hang of the corkscrew!
BRADLEY: I hope if they do a sequel they change it for something less awful!
BRADLEY: Yes... Anyway!
ALAN: If you wanna hear more stories, wheel in the next patient! I can cut AND chat!
ALAN: Probably...
ALAN: ...and that's pretty much the story!
ALAN: Dr. Bleed and I, we set up so many of our "Probe and Bleed" hospitals!
ALAN: Our students could operate anywhere and with anything!
ALAN: Then a new government came in, and shut us all down.
BRADLEY: I'm so sorry...
BRADLEY: I mean, Mr. Probe - I need to attend to some... issues. But I will keep a steady supply of patients coming your way!
ALAN: Heh! Just keep 'em coming!
ALAN: At last! Some quality time with a patient!
ALAN: So, tell me about yourself!
DICK MASTERSON: Well, when I was growing up I...
ALAN: I meant tell me about your weird injury or illness! The good stuff!
DICK: I have brittle bones. Every moment I live is agony.
ALAN: Sounds AMAZING! Let's start poking around!
ALAN: Well, your bones won't be giving you any more problems!
DICK: How come?
ALAN: You don't have any anymore. I replaced them all with metal junk I had laying around.
ALAN: So stay away from electrical equipment!
DICK: Ha ha ha! I like you! You're a kidder! I like kidders!
ALAN: No, seriously. If you as much as look at a microwave, you will die.
DICK: Ha ha! Classic bedside manner! Love it!
MISTRESS DISTRESS: So, "doctor"... I HEAR you're pretty "extreme"... But you LOOK a bit "old and past it" to me!
ALAN: I'll have you know I once saved the world from the Spider Queen's Evil Sisterhood using only my wits and a sparkly thong!
ALAN: No.Wait... That might not have been me...
DISTRESS: Ugh! Whatever! Well, I want you to take it to the next level!
DISTRESS: I want to be your surgical CANVAS OF AGONY! Paint me with pain!
GIMP: Yrrrrgh! Shrr rrrrrrryy rrrks prrrrn!
ALAN: Ewwww!
DISTRESS: Ahhh! That was amazing!
ALAN: I aim to please!
ALAN: Or at least not hit any vital organs...
GIMP: Wrrrrl yrrr strrrrple mrrr brrrrt-chrrrrks trrrgrrrtrrr?
ALAN: Uh... I don't use staples any more!
ALAN: Be seeing you!
ALAN: Well, that was strange and awful...
BRADLEY: Alan - Dr. Probe... How's it going?
ALAN: Ah ha ha! It's just like old times!
ALAN: Except Bleed is dead, I'm in a home and my family have all abandoned me...
ALAN: Bummer.
BRADLEY: Your family? Where are they?
ALAN: Ah... Classic story - young surgeon rockets to fame and fortune... Marries the vigilante girl of his dreams from the first game, has two kids...
ALAN: Then all his hospitals get shut down, he sinks into depression and drives his family crazy with his self-loathing, they leave...
ALAN: ...and he ends up in a home. That ol' chestnut!
BRADLEY: Wow...
ALAN: But doing surgery again is great! I feel I'm really starting to get the hang of it!
BRADLEY: That's excellent news! Please carry on - my... "contact" will be most pleased!
SCARLETT THROBBING: I have a medical condition, doc...
SCARLETT: ...but there's no easy way to say it!
ALAN: Is it one of those really long, latin words?
SCARLETT: Maybe... If "crustaceans" is latin...
ALAN: Oh!
SCARLETT: It's not what you think! I just ate some bad crab eggs is all!
SCARLETT: Oh that feels good!
ALAN: Steady! Just because my wife left me doesn't mean she couldn't walk in any minute!
SCARLETT: You're an amazing guy! So chivalrous!
ALAN: Yeah... That. Plus, I don't fancy operating on myself in the event of a heart attack.
ALAN: Bye now! Stay classy!
ALAN: AUUUUGH! OH GOD IT'S A CLOWN! A HORRIBLE, HORRIBLE CLOWN!
UNCLE CHUCKLES: *sigh* It's just like every kids' party I've ever done.
CHUCKLES: Are you going to scream and break my nose when I start pulling things out of my pants, too?
ALAN: Uh... Maybe!
ALAN: Sorry, I think I've got it under control now...
CHUCKLES: Right. Well, the problem is-
ALAN: AUGH! CLOWN!
ALAN: Sorry... Sorry.
ALAN: So, what's the problem?
CHUCKLES: Well, imagine pulling a rabbit out a hat...
CHUCKLES: ...except I didn't accidentally sit on my "rabbit" and get him stuck halfway up my "hat", if you get my drift...
ALAN: I wish this kind of thing was just a little bit less common...
CHUCKLES: Oh Mr. Bunny!
CHUCKLES: I know I say this every time, but I promise that will never happen again!
CHUCKLES: Thank you!
ALAN: AUGH! CLOWN!
CHUCKLES: I'm leaving.
ALAN: But hey! That's one of the advantages of the slight onset of senility!
MYSTERIOUS FIGURE: So, how is the good doctor progressing?
BRADLEY'S VOICE: He's a phenomenon!
BRADLEY: His technique is baffling - but when he gets into a flow...
BRADLEY: I can only imagine what he must have been like at the peak of his skills!
FIGURE: GOD! Why don't you just MARRY him if you love him so much?
BRADLEY: I didn't mean to get carried away sir...
FIGURE: Just make sure he'll be ready! I don't have much longer...
THE SHAME-ROCK: Ah, now but I know what you'll say, doc!
SHAME-ROCK: You'll say, "Ah, but that guy looks like he's been through the wars!", so you will!
ALAN: Actually, my guess would have been a blender, or possibly some kind of farm equipment...
SHAME-ROCK: Ha! But that's all part of being an elite cage-fighter, so it is!
SHAME-ROCK: And besides, you should have seen the other guy!
ALAN: Worse off, was he?
SHAME-ROCK: I literally broke me foot of in his a-
ALAN: Right! Well! Let's crack on, shall we?
SHAME-ROCK: Well, I feel great!
SHAME-ROCK: But is all the bandages exactly necessary?
ALAN: Well, you know how sometimes you have to put a cone on an injured dog to stop it hurting itself again because it's so stupid?
SHAME-ROCK: Ah! I see! This is to stop me from destroyin' me opponents too early on?
ALAN: Yes! Let's go with that explanation!
ALAN: But hey! That's one of the advantages of the slight onset of senility!
FIGURE: Gah! Bradley! Urk!
BRADLEY: Sir! We can't wait any longer! I'm going to get Alan!
FIGURE: Gack!
BRADLEY: Alan! It's time to go!
ALAN: I am all kinds of ready!
BRADLEY: Please put this blindfold on - we're going for a ride!
BRADLEY: We're here! You can take the blindfold off.
ALAN: Right!
ALAN: Uh... It's still dark! If you can just turn on some lights, I'll get to it!
BRADLEY: Ah... yes, about that... I'm afraid the identity of my employer can never be compromised.
ALAN: Meaning?
BRADLEY: Here's a torch.
BRADLEY: If you shine it anywhere near his face, I will be forced to shoot you.
ALAN: You ARE kidding me?
ALAN: Well, that was pretty crazy!
FIGURE: psstwsssspsss
BRADLEY: My employer says: "You'd better have gotten them all out this time".
ALAN: Tell him he's welcome - the ingrate!
ALAN: So, what's the deal now?
FIGURE: pssspspssp
BRADLEY: My employer says that he is extremely grateful.
BRADLEY: Is there anything you would like in return for your services?
ALAN: Heh. The only thing I really want is to be allowed to be a surgeon again!
ALAN: Legally, I mean.
ALAN: And for Aureola and the kids to come back.
ALAN: ...but I guess that ain't going to happen...
BRADLEY: Why don't you go and wait in the car, Alan? I will join you in a moment.
ALAN: Heh! Don't be long - the ol' bladder's not what it used to be! Heh heh!
BRADLEY: Well sir?
BRADLEY: Couldn't you pull some strings? He DID just save your life.
FIGURE: Indeed. Yes. I would like to do something for mister Probe.
FIGURE: Take him to a little place he can call his own - somewhere out of the way.
BRADLEY: You want me to set him up with an office? Oh sir - that's wonde-
FIGURE: Then shoot him in the back of the head.
BRADLEY: SIR! I-
FIGURE: You will leave now.
FIGURE: You can turn the lights on as you go.
FIGURE: I imagine the effect will be... Suitably dramatic.
BRADLEY: I...
BRADLEY: Yes sir...
BRADLEY: ...MISTER PRESIDENT!
THE PRESIDENT: DUM DUM DUUUUM!
VLADIMIR AMPIRE: Doktor! You must believe me!
VLADIMIR: I'm NOT a vampire! Those villagers got it all wrong!
ALAN: Uh... Ok. Let's see where this goes!
VLADIMIR: They staked me! They shot me with a silver bullet! They threw holy water at me, made me eat some garlic and almost cut my head off!
ALAN: Right... And you're still standing here, telling me you're NOT a vampire?
VLADIMIR: Yes! And it must be true, because any of those things vould have killed a vampire! Right?
ALAN: I'm pretty sure most humans would be dead too...
VLADIMIR: Regardless! You must fix me!
VLADIMIR: ...but bright light makes me explode into flame, so keep it dark, ok?
ALAN: So, are you SURE you're not a vampire? Why did villagers attack you?
ALAN: In fact, we're just outside a large city in the 21st century... Where did you even FIND villagers?
VLADIMIR: Vaat, vat is this? "Interview with the Vampire"?
VLADIMIR: Chillax already, baby!
ALAN: You ARE a vampire! Oh god!
ALAN: Does this mean you're going to suck me dry?
VLADIMIR: Ah! A double-entendre! Most excellent!
VLADIMIR: Thank you, no. But I think now I will throw up in my mouth a little.
VLADIMIR: Listen, here is a little token of my appreciation. If you ever find yourself dead, it may come in handy!
VLADIMIR: If it doesn't work... Just bring it back to me! Blah-ha-ha!
ALAN: He was a lot less sparkly than I'd expected...
ALAN: Wow... This place reminds me of the first place I ever performed surgery!
ALAN: It's ALMOST as much of a dump, anyway...
BRADLEY: Mister Probe...
BRADLEY: I have some bad news. I can't...
ALAN: Can't what? Can't wave a magic wand and reopen all my hospitals?
ALAN: I don't even care! I'm having a blast just doing what I do best!
ALAN: Tell you what - you can keep the patients coming. I'll treat 'em right here!
ALAN: Hey! Is there a patient in that chicken coop?
ALAN: Awww! An injured weirdo - just for me? You shouldn't have!
BRADLEY: I have no idea who that is...
BRADLEY: Listen, Alan...
BRADLEY: Something isn't right - it's not safe here...
ALAN: Not safe? That's great! Just like the glory days!
ALAN: Dr. Bleed and I, holed up in secret, dodging cops and criminals...
BRADLEY: But...
ALAN: Listen! I don't care what happened with your mysterious "employer"!
ALAN: All I want is to do surgery! I want to feel like my old self - my young self!
ALAN: So my family can be proud of me again...
BRADLEY: Your family?
BRADLEY: You really miss them, don't you?
BRADLEY: I'll see what I can do!
ALAN: Hooray! Just keep the patients coming!
NAPOLEON TROTTERSKI: MRRRRROINK!
ALAN: Well this is... Different.
NAPOLEON: SKWEEEE!
ALAN: What's that? Little Timmy fell down a well?
ALAN: Just kidding.
NAPOLEON: Skweee...
ALAN: A note...
ALAN: THIS is what you want me to do to you?
NAPOLEON: SANK YOU, MON DOCTOR.
NAPOLEON: COME LA REVOLUTIONE YOU WILL BE SPARED.
ALAN: Well thank goodness for that!
NAPOLEON: OR TURNED INTO GLUE. I AM NOT SURE YET.
CAP'N SPLINTERS: Yarrrgh! Doc! I haven't been feeling myself recently!
ALAN: Congratulations! I imagine it must be a real temptation on long, lonely sea voyages!
CAP'N: No! I mean I've been pillaging and violatin' my way across the seas for years - but now there's a heaviness on my heart!
ALAN: My salty old friend, it sounds like you've grown a conscience!
CAP'N: Yaarrrr, perhaps. But I was thinking it might be something to do with the live squid I swallowed?
CAP'N: Glaaaargh!
ALAN: Hmm. That is also a possibility...
CAP'N: Yarr... I almost miss him now he's gone...
ALAN: You know, I'm sure I already did a surgery just like this in one of the other "Amateur Surgeon" games...
ALAN: ...or maybe I was just playing a PIRATE copy! Ah ha ha ha!
CAP'N: ...
ALAN: ...
CAP'N: Yar. I'll just be goin', then.
PRINCESS STARLA: Oh doctor! Don't look at me!
ALAN: I wasn't! I mean, I might have caught a glimpse, but, well, you're quite... Revealed...
STARLA: Oh, you're so sweet! But I don't know how you can bear to even say such things!
STARLA: Won't you help me, please? I need you to make me beautiful!
ALAN: I can maybe do a little nip, slash, tuck here and there...
STARLA: Oh doctor!
STARLA: It's... I'm beautiful!
ALAN: I aim to please - or at least not hit any major vital organs!
ALAN: I may have used that line before...
ALAN: But hey! That's one of the advantages of the slight onset of senility!
ALAN: But hey! That's one of the advantages of the slight onset of senility!
PRESIDENT: Where the hell have you been?
PRESIDENT: The next words out of your mouth had better be "burying the evidence", or so help me!
BRADLEY: Uh...
BRADLEY: He escaped!
PRESIDENT: He's a 70-year-old man with a self-confessed bladder problem! How did he ESCAPE?
BRADLEY: Sir... please! What have you got against him?
BRADLEY: Your first act as president was to close his hospitals - you ruined his life!
BRADLEY: Then he SAVES yours, and you want him killed!
BRADLEY: Well I won't do it!
PRESIDENT: You WON'T? You won't obey a direct order from your PRESIDENT?
BRADLEY: He... He's my grandfather.
PRESIDENT: Your... Grandfather?
PRESIDENT: Ah... My dear boy... I had no idea.
PRESIDENT: Of course, this changes everything!
CHEWTOY CHUCK: Doc! Help me! I got one of my dogs too worked up during training! I've been bitten half to death!
ALAN: Hmm. Was it a German Shepherd?
CHUCK: What? No, you idiot! It was one of my DOGS.
ALAN: No, I meant... Never mind...
CHUCK: That's better! You know, I've got 100 dogs in all - all boys! Just one girl dog!
CHUCK: Funny things is, she's the ONLY one that doesn't bite me!
CHUCK: Guess you could say I've got ninety-nine problems, but the bitc-
ALAN: Ha... Well! Best let you go back to them!
JIMMY THE SPIDER: Ta daaaa! Look in your pocket! Your watch is gone! I stole it!
ALAN: Ah! What the hell are you supposed to be?
JIMMY: JIMMY THE SPIDER! Cat-burglar extraordinaire!
JIMMY: I must have burgled, oh, five cats so far! Ha ha ha!
ALAN: ...awful
JIMMY: No, I'm just kidding. But I am TOTALLY SPIDER-LIKE!
ALAN: And you want me to what, remove part of your brain to cure you of this weird behavior?
JIMMY: No way! It's just that recently, some of my SPIDER MEALS have been...
JIMMY: ...repeating on me.
ALAN: Gross... Well, say "Ah"!
JIMMY: Ah - that's so much better!
ALAN: Next time, why not try eating a spider to catch the flies?
ALAN: Or would that be like cannibalism?
JIMMY: Ha! Yeah! Sure - eat a spider! Can you imagine someone coming in with a whole bunch of spiders running around inside them?
ALAN: You'd be surprised...
BRADLEY: Alan! Listen - there's good news!
BRADLEY: I think something wonderful is going to happen! I think we're going to be able to reopen your hospitals!
ALAN: What? What?
BRADLEY: I'll explain more when you've finished with your next patient! Be quick!
ALAN: How can I hel-
PEEPING TOM: I LIKE TO LOOK AT THINGS.
TOM: Not necessarily WEIRD things, you understand!
ALAN: Just "things", hey? I see...
TOM: YOU "SEE"? YOU MOCK ME! YOU MOCK ME WITH YOUR BEAUTIFUL EYES! For I CANNOT SEE!
TOM: Or at least, not as well as I'd like to...
ALAN: One eyeball surgery coming right up!
TOM: Ahhh! It's wonderful!
TOM: I can't wait to start looking at some really WEIRD stuff again!
TOM: DON'T LOOK AT ME LIKE THAT! It makes me feel...dirty!
TOM: Actually, you CAN keep looking! Mmmm!
ALAN: Yeuch!
BRADLEY: Alan! My employer has had a change of heart!
BRADLEY: He wants to meet you! He has good news about your hospitals!
ALAN: Are you sure about this?
BRADLEY: Yes! Please - it's so important! I'll send a car - just be in it! Don't forget your blindfold!
ALAN: Hello? Bradley?
PRESIDENT: Hello Alan.
ALAN: Oh my God it's the president.
ALAN: You're the douche who closed all my hospitals!
ALAN: If I'd have known I was going to meet you, I'd have worn my open-back gown from the home, to make it easier to tell you to kiss my...
PRESIDENT: My dear, old, crazy Alan Probe...
PRESIDENT: How terribly you've aged!
ALAN: Screw this. Tell Bradley I'm out of here.
PRESIDENT: Why not tell him yourself?
BRADLEY: Groan...
ALAN: Oh my God. What did you DO?
PRESIDENT: Turns out little Bradley here was quite the keeper of secrets!
PRESIDENT: ...from both of us.
ALAN: Secrets? What? Is it the secret why you're SUCH a colossal douchebag?
PRESIDENT: Ah, it's so delicious - all the time you had with him, and you never knew!
PRESIDENT: Alan - he's your grandson!
ALAN: You're mental.
PRESIDENT: Ask him yourself, if you don't believe me.
PRESIDENT: You may need to patch him up a bit first!
ALAN: I just don't get it! What the hell have you got against me?
ALAN: You shut down my hospitals, rip up my legacy...
ALAN: And then you try to kill my possibly-grandson after I SAVE YOUR LIFE!
ALAN: WHAT is YOUR PROBLEM?
PRESIDENT: Ha! This, coming from the man who almost killed me with his botched surgery!
ALAN: I didn't botch anything!
PRESIDENT: The man who foiled my plans at every turn!
ALAN: I have done ZERO foiling! There has been NO FOILING!
PRESIDENT: The man who had me locked up for 20 years! 20 YEARS!
ALAN: I LOCKED YOU UP? WHAT THE HELL ARE YOU TALKING ABOUT?!
PRESIDENT: How I loathe you pizza boy!
PRESIDENT: How it gladdened me to see your dreams crumble!
PRESIDENT: For your family to abandon you, unable to witness your shame!
PRESIDENT: But now... To have your own grandson at my fingertips!
ALAN: Pizza boy? No-one's called me that for...
PRESIDENT: My one regret is that our stinking, worthless teacher DIED before he could see your downfall!
ALAN: Wait...
ALAN: OUR teacher? You mean, Dr. Bleed? But...
ALAN: Oh. My. God.
PRESIDENT: That's right, Alan!
PRESIDENT: MWAAAHAHAHAAAA!
ALAN: DWAYNE PIPE?
DWAYNE: DUM DUM DUMMMMM!
DWAYNE: How does it feel, pizza boy?
DWAYNE: To know that your old nemesis has WON!?
DWAYNE: I WIN!
DWAYNE: AH HAHAHAHAAAA!
ALAN: God you're a douche.
ALAN: I mean, wow. WOW.
DWAYNE: You even SAVED my LIFE!
DWAYNE: You pulled all those bugs out of me!
DWAYNE: You...
DWAYNE: BUUUURP
DWAYNE: Uh oh.
ALAN: AH HA HA HA!!! Looks like I missed a few!
ALAN: God, I SUCK at those bug extraction surgeries!
ALAN: Tick-tock, Dwayne!
ALAN: Tell you what, I'll save you if you promise to stop being a douche?
ALAN: Oh what the hell - one last time for old time's sake!
BRADLEY: Ugh... Alan, I'm so sorry.
BRADLEY: It's true. I am your grandson. I just wanted to bring our family back together...
BRADLEY: I thought if I became aide to the president, I could find a way to help you!
BRADLEY: I had no idea the president was the bad guy!
BRADLEY: I mean, how does a criminal imprisoned for MURDER manage to get out of jail, disguise his identity...
BRADLEY: ...and then run for, and become, president of the United States?
ALAN: Ha! Listen... grandson. We could sit here and poke holes in the plot all night...
ALAN: ...but I think I'd rather we went and visited some of our family, and started rebuilding some bridges! Whaddya say?
BRADLEY: Let's go... Grandpa!
ALAN: Ah, it'll be good to get out of this place!
ALAN: I've modified a pig, pulled a rabbit out of a clown, unmasked the president and gotten my life back...
ALAN: Not a bad run for an old guy who now sometimes forgets to drop his pants before he uses the toilet!
ALAN: I just wish Dr. Bleed was around to have seen it...
ALAN: Hey! Didn't that whack-job vampire say something about something that was something to do with raising the dead? Or something?
ALAN: I don't know if it's the joy in my heart at finishing the game, or the crazy in my head at there being nothing left to complete, but I think it's time to go pay an old friend a visit!
DR. IGNACIOUS BLEED: Hello Alan.
ALAN: Ha... Dr. Bleed! Oh wow... It worked!
ALAN: Ah... I want to say something witty to wind you up!
ALAN: But at the same time I almost want to cry... I've missed you!
BLEED: Ha! We made quite the team, didn't we?
ALAN: It was the best! Remember when I hit that old guy with the pizza van, and we patched him up together?
BLEED: Yes. That was me.
ALAN: Oh yeah!
ALAN: Man... I don't really know what to say now!
BLEED: You could apologize for digging me up?
ALAN: Yeah, I'm not really sure what I was thinking...
ALAN: I guess it's just not really an Amateur Surgeon game without Dr. Bleed!
BLEED: That's very kind of you Alan - I'm so very proud of you, and everything you've done.
ALAN: Even when I forgot to pull that thing out of Dwayne no fewer than three separate times?
BLEED: That was the best of all.
BLEED: I have to go now.
BLEED: Goodbye, Alan.
ALAN: Goodbye, Dr. Bleed...
